author: w3Lb
content: Plan refinements from design pass: (1) NO new dependency — encoding_rs cannot ENCODE to UTF-16 (WHATWG standard is decode-only for UTF-16); std covers everything: String::from_utf16 for decode, str::encode_utf16 for encode, manual BOM handling. (2) CRITICAL interaction found: UTF-16 text is full of NUL bytes, so the existing binary-detection (NUL in first 8KB) currently excludes UTF-16 files from discovery entirely — fix detection to exempt UTF-16 BOMs first. Implementation: new ripsed-fs encoding module (SourceEncoding {Utf8, Utf8Bom, Utf16Le, Utf16Be}, detect/decode/encode, string tags for serialization); reader::read_file_with_encoding; writer::write_atomic_encoded re-attaching BOM + re-encoding; discovery binary-check exempts FF FE / FE FF prefixes; UndoRecord gains optional encoding tag (serde default None = utf-8, back-compat with existing logs) so undo restores original bytes exactly; thread encoding through file/script/json modes + facade. Truncated UTF-16 (odd byte count) and unpaired surrogates -> clean io::Error, never panic. UTF-8 BOM: strip on read so patterns anchor correctly at line 1, re-attach on write.
